description: A well-formed nested dict literal in a dict-entry's separator position (`print { a { b: 1 } }` — `a` parses as a valid key, but the required `:` is missing and a nested, itself well-formed and balanced, `{ b: 1 }` appears instead) is a grammar-position error, not a brace-matching one: it reports exactly one ol-bad-token for the inner `{` — never ol-unmatched-brace — and both dict literals' braces, which are in fact correctly matched, close cleanly (spec/error-model.md, spec/data-structures.md#dictionaries, issue #546).
